channel.title: AI Firehose: What Is New
channel.description: We read the whole AI firehose so you do not have to, then distill it into one brief: what is new, what is breaking out, and what is worth your time, across the day, week, month, and quarter. You take what you want and leave the rest.

item.title: Ornith (35B,9B) + Hermes,Zed: THE FULLY PRIVATE LOCAL AGENT is ACTUALLY HERE!
item.description: AICodeKing: Ornith is a family of open agentic coding models from DeepReinforce, ranging from 9B to 397B parameters. Built on Gemma and Qwen, the models focus on coding, tool use, and agentic workflows with self-learned scaffolding for planning, retries, and testing. The 35B and 9B variants are practical for local deployment and support GGUF format.

item.title: How to Create a Client Pitch With AI — From Prompt to PDF in 15 Minutes
item.description: AI Master: This video demonstrates Venngage AI's one-pager generator, which automates the creation of client-ready marketing documents from a single prompt. The workflow covers content generation, design, editing, visual selection, and PDF export in approximately 15 minutes.

item.title: Agents in Production: How OpenGov Built and Scaled OG Assist - Gabe De Mesa, OpenGov
item.description: AI Engineer: Gabe De Mesa from OpenGov presents lessons from building and scaling OG Assist, an AI agent product serving thousands of state and local governments. The talk covers the core agent loop harness, feedback and evals, long context handling, monitoring, tool building, and production deployment best practices using Effect-TS, Claude, and Cursor.

item.title: A Genius With Amnesia - Victor Savkin, Nx
item.description: AI Engineer: Victor Savkin critiques coding agents for lacking persistent context and memory across interactions, limiting their effectiveness despite individual capability. He frames this as analogous to a perfect engineer with amnesia and proposes Nx and Polygraph as frameworks to address this constraint through better codebase visibility and agent autonomy.

item.title: Fable 5 Returns... This Is Big
item.description: AI Revolution: A YouTube video speculates that Anthropic's Fable 5 model may be returning based on leaked code strings, Amazon Bedrock listings, and Congressional pressure. The video frames potential re-release as connected to shifted U.S. relations with Anthropic and suggests a stronger Mythos successor is already in development.

item.title: The Log Is The Agent - Ishaan Sehgal, Omnara
item.description: AI Engineer: Sehgal argues that as LLMs commoditize, agent infrastructure differentiation will shift from orchestration layers and tools to durable session logs as the core primitive. He contends that logs enable agent persistence across models and runtimes, drawing an analogy to append-only log databases and video game save files.

item.title: Recursive Coding Agents - Raymond Weitekamp, OpenProse
item.description: AI Engineer: Raymond Weitekamp presents recursive language models that use inference-time compute patterns to improve coding agent performance and reliability. The approach maps recursion onto agent behavior for enhanced code generation and reasoning.

item.title: When Forgiveness Was a Trap - Ada Palmer
item.description: Dwarkesh Patel: Dwarkesh Patel interviews Ada Palmer on a podcast episode exploring the historical and philosophical dimensions of forgiveness. The title suggests an examination of how forgiveness functioned as a mechanism or constraint in particular social or historical contexts.

item.title: GTA VI + GPT-6: Rockstar's AI Mechanics Revealed
item.description: AI Master: A YouTube video argues that GTA VI does not use GPT-6 but instead employs Rockstar's proprietary AI stack including the RAGE 9 engine with witness systems, persistent NPC memory, and dynamic APB mechanics. The creator claims to have reviewed patents and job postings to counter viral misinformation about GTA VI AI capabilities and compares language model performance across GPT-6, Claude, and Gemini for research purposes.

item.title: What's new with data agents
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud Tech presented new data analytics agent capabilities including the Agent Development Kit (ADK) and Model Context Protocol (MCP) integrations. These tools enable building custom agents grounded in enterprise data and are accessible via Gemini CLI, Claude Code, Codex, and IDEs like VSCode.

item.title: Production Evals For Agentic AI Systems - Nishant Gupta, Meta Superintelligence Labs
item.description: AI Engineer: Nishant Gupta from Meta discusses production evaluation approaches for autonomous AI agents that go beyond offline benchmarks. The talk covers continuous evaluation pipelines, online vs offline evaluation architectures, human-in-the-loop systems, and methods for detecting drift and hallucinations in agentic workflows at scale.

item.title: Build Systems, Not Code - Angie Jones, Agentic AI Foundation
item.description: AI Engineer: Angie Jones argues that AI coding agents elevate software engineering from code-writing to system design. Engineering principles like decomposition, state management, and systems thinking remain essential but apply at a higher level of abstraction when building agentic systems.

item.title: Anthropic's Labs Lead On Fable's Capabilities + Building AI-Native Products — With Mike Krieger
item.description: Alex Kantrowitz: Mike Krieger, head of Anthropic Labs and Instagram co-founder, discusses building AI-native products with Fable and perspectives on Anthropic's safety approach. The interview covers how AI tooling is changing development workflows and Anthropic's competitive positioning as both platform and product.

item.title: Power intelligent agents with AI-native databases
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: AI-native databases enable intelligent agents to access enterprise data through AI-powered search, natural language interfaces, and multimodal queries. The session covers graph retrieval-augmented generation, Model Context Protocol, and agentic IDEs for agent deployment and context management.

item.title: Boost AI context with hybrid search in Spanner
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud Spanner now offers hybrid search combining full-text and vector search for AI applications. The system integrates with Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform to enable RAG and contextual search experiences built on operational data.

item.title: Agent development and AgentOps with BigQuery, ADK, and MCP
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud presented Agent Development Kit (ADK) and Model Context Protocol (MCP) integration methods for standardizing how agents connect to data sources like BigQuery, Google Maps, and AlloyDB. The session covered building agents with a single workflow and implementing AgentOps for observability and cost monitoring with minimal code.

item.title: Build connected AI: Orchestrate tools and agents with registries and ADK
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud presented the Agent Development Kit (ADK) with Agent Registry for securely connecting agents to organizational data sources and other agents. The session covers integrating Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers, building custom MCP servers, and transforming APIs into agent-ready tools using specification boost add-on.

item.title: What's new with Gemini from Google DeepMind
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google DeepMind unveiled Gemini 3 with upgraded model reasoning, expanded context window processing, and enhanced multimodal tool use capabilities. The announcement showcases new workflows for building agentic applications through Vertex AI integration and highlights research advancements driving agent design.

item.title: Building an AI app: A low-code guide for small teams
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud demonstrates a low-code approach to building AI applications by integrating Gemini with corporate databases like Cloud SQL and AlloyDB for PostgreSQL. The platform enables rapid development of intelligent features including natural language interfaces, hybrid search, image search, and recommendations without requiring specialized AI expertise.

item.title: What's new in Looker: Empowering business users in the governed agentic era
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud announced advancements in Looker, integrating Gemini AI to create dashboard agents that automate workflows and insights. The updates include conversational analytics, semantic model evolutions, and embedded analytics capabilities grounded in a governed semantic layer for enterprise use.

item.title: NoSQL for modern apps and AI: The future of Memorystore, Firestore, and Bigtable
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud presented advancements in Memorystore, Firestore, and Bigtable NoSQL databases with features designed for AI and modern application workloads. The session covered product releases, architectural innovations, and roadmaps for agentic and AI app experiences.

item.title: Implementing DeepMind innovation: Deep research API
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google DeepMind presents Deep research API, powered by Gemini, enabling agents that enhance performance on comprehensive research tasks. The session demonstrates how to implement these agent-based techniques using Google Cloud infrastructure.

item.title: What's new in Cloud Run
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud Run is a serverless platform optimized for deploying AI agent and inference workloads with automatic scaling and reduced operational overhead. New capabilities focus on simplifying application deployment and supporting next-generation AI-driven development.

item.title: What's new in Cloud SQL: Drive performance, high availability, and security
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud announced new AI-driven capabilities for Cloud SQL supporting MySQL, PostgreSQL, and SQL Server, including automated health management, fleet-wide insights, and enhanced performance, availability, and security. Database Migration Service facilitates low-risk cloud migrations for optimized database strategy.

item.title: What's new in Google Cloud's agent platform
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud unveiled its unified Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform with a no-code/low-code Agent Designer for building multi-agent workflows. The platform includes new Agent Registry and marketplace for component distribution, enterprise governance layer with observability, time-travel debugging, evaluation suite, and managed simulation platform. Features include expanded Agent Development Kit with language support and secure code execution sandbox.

item.title: The Miranda Hypothesis: How Hamilton Poisoned Persona Evals - Jacob E. Thomas, Results Gen
item.description: AI Engineer: Jacob E. Thomas argues that persona-eval benchmarks systematically fail to detect when character-based AI systems output culturally dominant composites rather than documentary-grounded reasoning, a failure mode he names Miranda distortion. He proposes epistemic simulation with historian-in-the-loop evaluation and describes the pre-registered Prism Experiment as a methodological solution using temporally-anchored diagnostic questions and domain expert rubrics.

item.title: What's new for AI on GKE: Training, serving, and agents
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud presented GKE innovations for AI workloads including training, serving, and agentic workflows. The platform has been updated to support reinforcement learning and complex agent-based systems with improved reliability, scalability, and cost-effectiveness. Companies like Thinking Machines Lab and Baseten are using GKE for production AI workloads.

item.title: Engineering the future of Kubernetes for AI at scale
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud Tech session on optimizing Kubernetes for AI workloads including agents, inference, and batch processing. Focuses on hardware-aware platform architecture, infrastructure elasticity, securing agentic workflows, and intelligent resource optimization.

item.title: Unsure how to approach CS50? Carter Zenke answers.
item.description: CS50: CS50 is Harvard University's introduction to computer science and programming, available through edX, Harvard Extension School, and Harvard Summer School. The course is taught by David J. Malan and maintains active communities across Discord, GitHub, Reddit, and other platforms.

item.title: Kimi K2.7 Code: THIS is VERY CRAZY MAN!
item.description: AICodeKing: Moonshot AI released Kimi K2.7 Code, an open-source coding-focused agentic model with a Mixture-of-Experts architecture, one trillion parameters, and 256K context window. The accompanying Kimi Code CLI enables terminal-based repository exploration, file editing, command execution, and testing with plan mode for codebase investigation.

item.title: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 Day 3 Livestream
item.description: AI Engineer: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 held its final day of main-stage programming on July 2, 2026 at Moscone West in San Francisco. The livestream featured keynotes, talks, and a startup battlefield competition, serving as a major industry gathering point.

item.title: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 Day 2 Livestream
item.description: AI Engineer: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 is a conference held at Moscone West in San Francisco on July 1, 2026. The event features keynote sessions and main-stage programming throughout the day, livestreamed online.

item.title: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 Day 1: Software Factories & Keynotes
item.description: AI Engineer: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 took place on June 30, 2026 at Moscone West in San Francisco. Day 1 featured keynote sessions and main-stage programming on software factories and related topics, livestreamed for online viewers.

item.title: Anthropic accuses Alibaba of illicitly extracting AI capabilities - BBC
item.description: Google News AI: Anthropic has accused Alibaba of illicitly extracting AI capabilities, according to a BBC report. The accusation suggests unauthorized access to or replication of AI model functionality.

item.title: Ask HN: Where is our profession (programmer) going?
item.description: syntaxbush: A programmer observes that at a visited software company, LLMs like Claude have become the primary tool for code generation and problem-solving, with human code review, abstraction practices, and deep problem understanding being de-emphasized. The author expresses concern that this represents a shift from precise, knowledge-intensive work toward probabilistic, outsourced decision-making.

item.title: The Agentic OS Setup That Will 10x Claude Code
item.description: Chase AI: Chase AI presents an "Agentic OS" framework for building systems around Claude Code that incorporates loop engineering, memory management, and skills architecture. The approach emphasizes that mastering agentic OS fundamentals improves Claude Code projects through customization, visual interfaces, and shareable packages for non-technical teams.

item.title: [AINews] It's Meta-Harness Summer
item.description: Latent Space: Latent Space discusses a shift toward Meta's harness approaches for evaluating AI systems, suggesting Meta-based testing harnesses are becoming the dominant standard in the industry. The piece presents this as a significant trend shift in AI evaluation methodology.

item.title: Bible as RAG Database
item.description: jacksonastone: A discussion or exploration of using the Bible as a retrieval-augmented generation database. This applies RAG techniques to religious text retrieval and reference systems.

item.title: What I'm Finding About LLM Code Style and Token Costs
item.description: jimmont: The author reports observations about how different code styles affect token consumption when using LLMs. This is a practical analysis of code formatting patterns and their impact on inference cost, based on experimental findings.

item.title: Europe is pushing back on Washington’s chip war
item.description: TechCrunch AI: Europe is resisting U.S. export restrictions on semiconductor manufacturing equipment as part of Washington's broader strategy to contain Chinese chip capabilities. ASML CEO notes that current Chinese access is limited to older deep ultraviolet lithography tools from a decade ago, which the MATCH Act would further restrict.

item.title: We'll fight the platform war against Big AI
item.description: aendruk: An author signals intent to challenge the dominance of large AI platforms and incumbent companies. The framing suggests a platform competition dynamic between centralized AI incumbents and alternative approaches.

item.title: Google Just Dropped a Masterclass on Agentic Engineering (It's SO Good)
item.description: Cole Medin: Cole Medin breaks down Google's whitepaper on agentic engineering, arguing that the AI model is only 10% of success while the surrounding harness (context, tools, guardrails, verification) comprises the critical 90%. The video frames AI coding as a spectrum from vibe coding to agentic engineering and emphasizes skills in context engineering, harness development, and the shift from conductor to orchestrator roles.

item.title: simonw/browser-compat-db
item.description: Simon Willison: Simon Willison created simonw/browser-compat-db, a SQLite database conversion of Mozilla's browser compatibility data. The project includes Claude-generated Python scripts for data transformation, a GitHub Actions workflow built with GPT-4.5, and makes the database publicly accessible via GitHub CDN with CORS support for exploration via Datasette Lite.

item.title: What we learned from 1,604 Chinese AI job postings
item.description: Epoch AI: Epoch AI analyzed 1,604 Chinese AI job postings to infer the research strategies and priorities of Chinese AI labs. The analysis uses hiring patterns as a window into organizational focus areas and technology direction.

item.title: Former Infosys chief has a new startup that wants to challenge the IT services world
item.description: TechCrunch AI: Former Infosys CEO Vishal Sikka has launched a new startup backed by Mayfield and Aramco Ventures. The company brings together veterans from SAP, Infosys, and VianAI to challenge the traditional IT services sector.

item.title: Anthropic Just Replaced Claude Code With New Claude Tag
item.description: AI Revolution: Anthropic launched Claude Tag, a team version of Claude Code integrated into Slack for connecting company tools and managing tasks in shared channels. It replaces the previous Claude Slack chatbot.

item.title: Omnigent: Open-source meta harness for agents
item.description: chatmasta: Omnigent is an open-source framework that provides a meta harness for agents. It appears designed to standardize or simplify the development and deployment of AI agent systems.

item.title: Cerebras stock plunges after earnings as CEO says margin outlook was misunderstood
item.description: TechCrunch AI: Cerebras, an AI chipmaker, reported lower-than-expected gross margins in its first earnings as a public company, causing its stock price to decline. The CEO attributed the market reaction to a misunderstanding of the company's margin outlook.

item.title: A24 Knows You're Mad About the Google AI Collab
item.description: nate: A24 is facing backlash from its audience over a collaboration with Google AI. The announcement has generated negative public sentiment regarding the entertainment company's partnership with the tech giant.

item.title: AI was supposed to kill engineering jobs, but new data suggests they’re the most resilient
item.description: TechCrunch AI: TechCrunch reports on SignalFire data showing that engineering jobs are resilient despite predictions of AI-driven displacement. Engineers represent a larger share of new hires in AI-affected sectors, contradicting narratives about widespread job elimination.
